title: The Boat

jokes.text: Why?
jokes.setup: Oscar asks for sensitivity, maturity, and discretion regarding his affair with the Senator
jokes.punchline: Single word response that undermines the entire dramatic request
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language - one word completely deflates Oscar's dramatic confession. The structure is flawless setup/punchline.

jokes.text: I'm not a day trader. I'm just a first responder in a disaster.
jokes.setup: Character is handling Andy's family financial crisis
jokes.punchline: Reframes financial advisor role as emergency response
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that's character-specific and original. Good structure but not exceptional impact.

jokes.text: his brother, Walt, Jr., locked himself in the wine cellar
jokes.setup: Describing the family meltdown aftermath
jokes.punchline: Rich family member's response to crisis is hiding in wine cellar
jokes.explanation: Solid absurd detail about wealthy family dysfunction, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Are the Nards hurting? Yeah, you bet. Got kicked pretty hard. Family's shattered, supersad. But I'm kind of crushing it in the damage-control department.
jokes.setup: Acknowledging family tragedy
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to bragging about his own performance
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe character moment - shows complete tone-deafness and narcissism in crisis.

jokes.text: I wish my dad could see me now. Of course, he caused this whole mess, so him.
jokes.setup: Wishing for father's approval of his crisis management
jokes.punchline: Remembering his father caused the crisis and cursing him
jokes.explanation: Great structure - builds expectation then immediately subverts it. Shows character's emotional confusion well.

jokes.text: I don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: Oscar gives Kevin a dramatic line about remembering something
jokes.punchline: Kevin's honest confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his literal-mindedness cuts through Oscar's drama.

jokes.text: Yeah, she hosts The Dr. Laura Show. Nope, that's Dr. Laura.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about radio host Iris Black
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction of obviously wrong answer
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline but fairly basic workplace confusion humor.

jokes.text: I do! I do! I do! I do! I do!
jokes.setup: Asked who feels passionate about local business issues
jokes.punchline: Dwight's over-enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight overeagerness, perfectly in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yeah, wow. Ten reams of 40-pound bond at only $6.90 after discount? Uh, whatever you say, Brad Pitt!
jokes.setup: Dwight explains how easy it is to sell things when you're famous
jokes.punchline: His example involves mundane paper sales with celebrity worship
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - Dwight's fantasy involves the most mundane possible celebrity endorsement.

jokes.text: Which place? The one right near Philly? I could be there in an hour.
jokes.setup: Learning Andy's brother is in rehab
jokes.punchline: Meredith immediately shows predatory interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith moment - shows her complete lack of boundaries and opportunistic nature.

jokes.text: Greta, the tittle-tattle, prattled on about the little metal bottle. Oh, my God. She spat a bit of spittle on the mottled brittle cattle in a bitter battle...
jokes.setup: Dwight practicing tongue twisters for radio interview
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd alliterative phrases
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showcase for Dwight's overpreparation, but gets repetitive.

jokes.text: Stop questioning my methods, okay? I was chosen for this task for a very good reason. No. You chose yourself.
jokes.setup: Dwight claims he was chosen for the radio interview
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim/Dwight dynamic - Jim deflates Dwight's self-importance with simple truth.

jokes.text: Totally, except it is a no-go. This boat was the heart and soul of our family, so... What else we got? Non-boat ideas.
jokes.setup: Andy explains the boat sale would solve everything
jokes.punchline: Immediately rejects it for sentimental reasons then moves on casually
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's emotional attachments conflicting with practical needs.

jokes.text: She could get, like, 60 packages of ramen noodles for five bucks. She could eat for a month.
jokes.setup: Discussing expensive silverware value
jokes.punchline: Kevin's understanding of economics and nutrition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - reduces complex financial situation to simple, wrong math.

jokes.text: I worked at a Jiffy Lube. See? I bet you wouldn't sell that Jiffy Lube for all the money in the world, would you, Darryl? I would if I owned it and I went broke.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to relate to Darryl about sentimental places
jokes.punchline: Darryl's practical, deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Perfect class divide joke - Andy's privileged sentimentality vs Darryl's working-class pragmatism.

jokes.text: What are you going to do? Cry about it? Excuse me.
jokes.setup: Andy gives tough-guy speech about not crying
jokes.punchline: Immediately leaves to cry
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate contradiction visual gag - says he won't cry, immediately goes to cry.

jokes.text: Well, why don't I go and tell Dwight so he can stop being such a complete nipple?
jokes.setup: Jim offering to tell Dwight the interview is cancelled
jokes.punchline: Uses unusual insult 'nipple'
jokes.explanation: Mild Jim insult with unusual word choice. Decent character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: We cannot let that happen.
jokes.setup: Jim says Dwight will be disappointed about cancelled interview
jokes.punchline: Pam's mock-serious response about protecting Dwight's feelings
jokes.explanation: Pam playing along with prank setup, mild reaction beat humor.

jokes.text: This isn't how I would cheer up just anyone, but it's a girlfriend's job to know her man, and I know Andy. He is seriously juvenile. He's like 40, though, right? Oh, no. He couldn't be more than, like, thirties, tops.
jokes.setup: Erin explaining she knows Andy is juvenile and age-appropriate activities work
jokes.punchline: Doesn't actually know how old he is
jokes.explanation: Good Erin moment - claims to know Andy well but demonstrates she doesn't know basic facts.

jokes.text: Help! My teeth are all messed up in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Andy has fake vampire teeth in during serious business discussion
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact description of the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist visual - the mundane description of obviously fake teeth is funny.

jokes.text: Oh, really, Angela? That's interesting. Because I do think that I could surprise you. I think that I could surprise you all the... Ya! I have to go to the bathroom!
jokes.setup: Kevin building up to reveal his big secret about Angela
jokes.punchline: Bails out with bathroom excuse at crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with character-appropriate chickening out. Kevin's cowardice is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That actually wasn't the worst cover. I'd say at least once a week Kevin runs out of the room shouting that he has to go to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Kevin just used bathroom excuse to avoid revealing secret
jokes.punchline: Angela reveals this is Kevin's regular pattern
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds depth to Kevin's character - turns one-time gag into running pattern.

jokes.text: All I ever wanted to do was sail the damn thing. But Dad wouldn't let me. He said, 'You can't be a skipper until you're a man.' You know, I'd reach for the wheel and he'd just smack my hand away.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his relationship with the family boat
jokes.punchline: Reveals abusive father preventing him from sailing
jokes.explanation: Good character depth moment with dark undertones - explains Andy's attachment and daddy issues.

jokes.text: Does your shirt have buttons? Yes. I'm so sorry. We are going to have to ask you to remove the shirt altogether.
jokes.setup: Radio host asking about clinking sounds from Dwight's end
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to removing shirt entirely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - goes from minor sound issue to complete shirt removal absurdly quickly.

jokes.text: Your voice, it's sounding a little feminine. That's impossible. Are you by any chance wearing pants with a metallic zipper?
jokes.setup: Radio host continues finding problems with Dwight's audio
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and ridiculous audio 'solutions'
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of escalation - the specificity of 'metallic zipper' is absurd.

jokes.text: he just wanted a little Mexican brought in. I can't! It's too much! Please stop.
jokes.setup: Oscar innocently talking about the Senator ordering Mexican food
jokes.punchline: Kevin can't handle the unintentional sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe moment - Kevin's awareness of innuendo Oscar doesn't realize he's making creates perfect discomfort.

jokes.text: Yeah, Oscar's in big trouble.
jokes.setup: Kevin just learned about Oscar's affair with Angela's husband
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement from Kevin - captures the enormity through his simple assessment.

jokes.text: His gambling problem must have resurfaced. I'm going to have to send him home until I can do an investifafion.
jokes.setup: Angela explaining why Kevin needs to go home
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'investigation' as 'investifafion'
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment with Angela's occasional malapropisms, but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Unless it's exposed to oxygen. Then it becomes extremely toxic.
jokes.setup: Paper mill foreman asked if paper is toxic, says no
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself with absurd caveat
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with perfect timing. The contradiction is immediate and absurd - paper toxic when exposed to oxygen is brilliantly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Get out of here, moron! Okay! Excuse me, Mr. Dwight, who are you talking to? Uh... No one.
jokes.setup: Dwight yelling at someone off-camera during live radio
jokes.punchline: Radio host catches him and he lies poorly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Dwight getting caught being unprofessional on live radio.

jokes.text: 'Cause then I would get my own office. And I wouldn't screw up your secret with Angela. I've been really worried about that.
jokes.setup: Kevin hoping for promotion
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he's been worried about keeping Oscar's secret
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment showing his inability to actually keep secrets while claiming he can.

jokes.text: Those figures I gave you? They're false. No. I was mad at Kevin. We had a fight, and I acted vindictively. So you set him up. Yes. He's innocent.
jokes.setup: Angela confessing to framing Kevin
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of vindictive behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angela's petty vindictiveness and willingness to admit it.

jokes.text: A few years ago, when I was on the jury, the Scranton Strangler? Sure. I always thought he might have been set up, but I felt pressured to convict. Well, that's got to be tough. Tough? I put an innocent man on death row.
jokes.setup: Kevin connecting Angela setting him up to a bigger conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he thinks he sent innocent man to death row
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and callback - Kevin's logic connecting small office dispute to major criminal case is perfectly absurd and dark.

jokes.text: Listen, the person responsible for this catastrophe is the CEO and Chairman, David Wallace!
jokes.setup: Dwight being asked about resignation over radio disaster
jokes.punchline: Throws David Wallace under the bus on live radio
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's radio disaster - perfectly in character to blame everyone else.

jokes.text: So this is how your family came to America. Move. Sorry? I'm trying to rig a boat here. I don't know how to do that when you're standing in the way. I'm not a ghost, so I can't walk through people.
jokes.setup: Erin making innocent comment about boat, captain annoyed
jokes.punchline: Captain's overly literal explanation of why he can't work around her
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan humor - the ghost comment is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Don't do that. Oh, it's okay. My girlfriend and I were actually gonna take her for a little spin and a picnic before you guys head out tonight. You know what? Get some dinner, on me... No. Nope. Can't do that.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to negotiate time on his own boat
jokes.punchline: Captain repeatedly shutting him down despite Andy owning the boat
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation - Andy losing control of his own boat sale creates mounting frustration.

jokes.text: Wow! I can smack you all day if you keep touching what you're not supposed to touch.
jokes.setup: Captain getting increasingly aggressive with Andy
jokes.punchline: Threatens physical violence over boat touching
jokes.explanation: Escalation continues with captain's aggression, good for building tension but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Damn it! Screw you, Dad!
jokes.setup: Andy's mounting frustration with boat situation
jokes.punchline: Blames absent father for current predicament
jokes.explanation: Good character moment tying current frustration to deeper family issues.

jokes.text: Andy, you never had to sail the boat to be a man. Fine, but I could have. As long as we're on this boat, as far as I'm concerned, you're the captain. I am the captain. Yeah. Right? Yeah! I'm the captain.
jokes.setup: Erin trying to comfort Andy about never sailing
jokes.punchline: Andy's growing confidence/delusion about being captain
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Andy's eventual decision to sail away - shows his building confidence.

jokes.text: For those just joining us, terror in Greenwich, where the police have surrounded the house of Dunder Mifflin CEO David Wallace. Wallace is said to be despondent over the company's stock plummet, and has taken a mailman hostage.
jokes.setup: Dwight's radio interview has caused stock problems
jokes.punchline: Situation has escalated to David Wallace taking hostages
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - takes Dwight's radio mishap to absurd logical extreme with hostage situation.

jokes.text: I think it was yesterday. I thought you were in rehab. Yeah, I just, uh... I figured I'd get that first relapse out of the way.
jokes.setup: Andy finds his brother who was supposed to be in rehab
jokes.punchline: Walt's casual attitude toward relapse as inevitable
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - treating relapse as an inevitable chore to get done is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: but I've decided to sail it to Bermuda. Bahamas, Andy. Same thing.
jokes.setup: Andy announces sailing plan to Bermuda
jokes.punchline: Gets destination wrong, dismisses correction casually
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's obliviousness and privilege - treating Caribbean islands as interchangeable.

jokes.text: But David, listen to me carefully. I'm going to need you to let the mailman go, okay? Walk out of the house with your hands on top of your head. Everything is going to be fine. Dunder Mifflin will be in good hands while you're away in prison.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to help David Wallace in hostage situation
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption that David will go to prison
jokes.explanation: Great escalation continuation - Dwight's matter-of-fact assumption about prison time while trying to be helpful.

jokes.text: Overall, I'd say my first radio interview went pretty much the way I expected.
jokes.setup: Dwight's radio interview caused stock crash and CEO hostage situation
jokes.punchline: Claims it went as expected
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight moment - perfect deadpan understatement after complete catastrophe. Shows his complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Oh, I just had an intuition that someone I love needed a little of my attention today. Oscar! Looking very healthy. Getting lots of vigorous exercise?
jokes.setup: Senator arrives unexpectedly at office
jokes.punchline: Makes loaded comment about Oscar's 'exercise'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with obvious innuendo that creates uncomfortable tension.

jokes.text: Oscar is having sex with the Senator and Angela doesn't even know. Her life is a complete sham!
jokes.setup: Kevin has been trying to keep Oscar's secret
jokes.punchline: Immediately spills everything to camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - after all his worry about keeping the secret, tells everyone via documentary camera.

jokes.text: Would I have gone with him if he'd asked me? On his sailboat cruise to the Caribbean? Yes. I think that would have been really fun and romantic.
jokes.setup: Erin helped Andy achieve his dream of sailing away
jokes.punchline: Reveals she would have loved to go but wasn't invited
jokes.explanation: Good bittersweet moment showing Erin's selflessness and Andy's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Does he have a flipper? Oh. Nope. It's not that. He, uh... He flipped a table one time when he was drunk. He sounds like an idiot. Yeah, he is.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his friend nicknamed 'Flipper'
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic explanation followed by agreement friend is an idiot
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with misdirection. Pete's honest assessment of his friend is endearing.

tmdb_overview: Andy faces family financial troubles; Dwight appears on radio; Kevin discovers Oscar's secret.
